content.text: Dynamics is the branch of mechanics that deals with the motion of objects and the forces causing that motion.
A force is a push or pull that can change the state of rest or motion of a body. SI unit: Newton (N).
Types: Contact forces (friction, tension, normal) and Non-contact forces (gravitational, electric, magnetic).
First Law (Inertia): A body remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force.

content.name: Newton's Second Law
content.note: The rate of change of momentum is directly proportional to the applied force.

content.text: Third Law: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
In an isolated system, total momentum remains constant. Example: collisions and explosions.
